title: Rails controllers
description: Completion of a Rails controller action — one event per HTTP request handled by the app. Carries the full request timing breakdown (total duration, plus db_runtime and view_runtime as components), the route/controller/action, and the response status.

action.description: Action method on the controller, e.g. "index", "destroy".

path.description: Request path, e.g. "/follows". Often high-cardinality due to ids.

status.description: HTTP status code returned to the client. Type-hint as ::int. Filter >= 500 for server errors, >= 400 for any error.

duration.description: Total wall-clock time the action took, in milliseconds. Includes db_runtime and view_runtime.

db_runtime.description: Milliseconds spent in DB queries during this action.

view_runtime.description: Milliseconds spent rendering views during this action.

request_id.description: Rails request UUID — also present on sibling sql.active_record, render_*.action_view, and cache_*.active_support events from the same request.

instrumenter_id.description: ActiveSupport::Notifications instrumenter UUID, added by the Honeybadger notification subscriber.
